overview.description: GitHub Marketplace connects developers with tools that extend and improve GitHub workflows. Two types of tools can be listed: GitHub Actions and Apps (GitHub Apps or OAuth Apps). GitHub Apps are the officially recommended way to integrate.
overview.key_distinction: GitHub Actions can be published by anyone. Apps can be listed for free by anyone, but only apps owned by organizations can sell (offer paid plans). Paid apps require publisher verification.

submission_process_steps.title: Review app against best practices and requirements
submission_process_steps.details: Ensure the app follows security best practices, customer experience guidelines, and meets all listing requirements. Apps must provide value beyond just authentication.

submission_process_steps.title: Add webhook events for billing
submission_process_steps.details: Configure webhook events to track user billing requests using the GitHub Marketplace API. Handle marketplace_purchase events for new purchases, upgrades, downgrades, cancellations, and free trials.

submission_process_steps.title: Create a draft GitHub Marketplace listing
submission_process_steps.details: Navigate to https://github.com/marketplace/new or through Developer Settings → GitHub Apps/OAuth Apps → select app → 'List in Marketplace'. The app must be public. Only organization owners can create/submit listings for organization-owned apps.

submission_process_steps.title: Add pricing plans
submission_process_steps.details: Configure free, flat-rate, or per-unit pricing plans. Up to 10 plans allowed. Paid plans must support both monthly and annual billing in USD. Free trials of 14 days can be offered.

submission_process_steps.title: Complete listing metadata
submission_process_steps.details: Fill out listing name, very short description (40-80 chars recommended), introductory description (150-250 chars), detailed description (up to 1000 chars), categories, supported languages, URLs (customer support, privacy policy, installation URL for OAuth), logo (min 200x200px, transparent background), feature card (965x482px), and up to 5 product screenshots (min 1200px wide).

submission_process_steps.title: Accept GitHub Marketplace Developer Agreement
submission_process_steps.details: Read and accept the terms of the GitHub Marketplace Developer Agreement.

submission_process_steps.title: Submit listing for review
submission_process_steps.details: From the Overview page of the draft listing, click 'Request publish'. An onboarding expert will reach out with additional information about the onboarding process.

all_listings.description: Requirements that apply to both free and paid apps
items: Must provide value to the GitHub community
Must accept GitHub Marketplace Developer Agreement
Only organization owners can create/submit listings for organization-owned apps
Must not actively persuade users away from GitHub
Must include valid contact information for the publisher
Must have a relevant description of the application
Must specify a pricing plan
Must have a valid link to a privacy policy
Must provide a support method (valid support link and/or support email)
All additional links must work and resolve to relevant pages
Must integrate with the platform beyond just authentication
Must be publicly available (no public preview or invite-only)
Must have webhook events set up for plan changes/cancellations via Marketplace API

brand_and_listing.description: Brand and listing requirements for all apps
items: GitHub logo usage must follow GitHub Logos and Usage guidelines
Must have logo, feature card, and screenshots meeting recommended guidelines
Descriptions must be well-written and free of grammatical errors
Free apps are encouraged in GitHub Marketplace
If a paid version exists outside Marketplace, once meeting paid app requirements, at least one paid plan must be offered in Marketplace

paid_apps.description: Requirements for paid apps (applies when offering paid plans)
items: App must be owned by an organization that is a verified publisher
GitHub Apps must have a minimum of 100 installations
OAuth apps must have a minimum of 200 users
Must handle Marketplace purchase events for new purchases, upgrades, downgrades, cancellations, and free trials
Must support both monthly and annual billing for paid subscriptions
May offer any combination of free and paid plans

publisher_verification.purpose: Required to offer paid plans or display a marketplace badge
prerequisites: App must be owned by an organization (not a personal account)
Must have owner permissions in the organization
Enable two-factor authentication for the organization
Verify a domain for the organization
Ensure basic profile information is present and accurate with a valid support email
process: Go to the organization's Settings → Developer settings → Publisher Verification
Complete the checklist (profile info, 2FA, verified domain)
GitHub reviews the details and notifies when verification is complete

listing_name.description: Appears on Marketplace homepage. Can differ from app's name. Cannot match an existing GitHub account name (unless it's your own).

guidelines: Describe app's functionality, not a call to action

categories.primary: Required - best category for the app

supported_languages.description: Programming languages the app works with (optional)

recommendations: Choose badge background color matching logo background

feature_card.description: Background image for featured spot on homepage. Should capture brand personality.

guidelines: Show as much UI as possible
Exclude browser chrome (address bar, title bar, toolbar)
Use level-three headers (###) for value proposition titles

paid_plan_requirements: Must set price for both monthly and yearly subscriptions in USD
Must request verification to publish with a paid plan
Can offer 14-day free trial that auto-converts to paid subscription
notes: Once a pricing plan is published for an already-listed app, it cannot be edited. Must be deleted and re-created.
Existing customers on a removed plan continue on it until they cancel.
Cannot list with a free plan if a paid service exists outside Marketplace.

approval_process.submission: Click 'Request publish' from the Overview page of the draft listing after completing all required sections.
approval_process.review: An onboarding expert reviews the submission and contacts the publisher with additional information.
post_approval: Can edit information and images after approval
Cannot change existing published pricing plans (must delete and recreate)
Monthly payments received for paid app purchases
metrics_available: GitHub Marketplace Insights page tracks app performance metrics
Transactions page allows download of all transactions

api_and_webhooks.api: REST endpoints for GitHub Marketplace API available at docs.github.com

key_restrictions.organization_ownership: Only organization owners can create/submit listings for organization-owned apps. GitHub App manager role does NOT grant listing permission.
key_restrictions.public_app_only: Only public apps can have draft listings created.
key_restrictions.personal_accounts: Personal accounts cannot sell apps. Apps must be transferred to an organization for paid plans.
key_restrictions.free_plan_restriction: Cannot list a free plan if a paid service exists outside Marketplace (with some exceptions after meeting paid app requirements).
